Class ApplyStrategy

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.prover.engine.AbstractProverCore<Proof,Goal>
org.key_project.prover.engine.impl.DefaultProver<Proof,Goal>
de.uka.ilkd.key.prover.impl.ApplyStrategy
All Implemented Interfaces:
ProverCore<Proof,Goal>
public class ApplyStrategy extends DefaultProver<Proof,Goal>
Applies rules in an automated fashion. The caller should ensure that the strategy runs in its own thread Uses code by Hans Muller and Kathy Walrath

  • Constructor Details

  • Method Details

    • start

      public ApplyStrategyInfo<Proof,Goal> start(Proof proof, Goal goal)
      Description copied from interface: ProverCore
      Starts a proof search for the given goal using the specified proof instance and the strategy settings provided by the proof instance.
      Parameters:
      proof - The ProofObject representing the proof instance.
      goal - The ProofGoal to prove.
      Returns:
      An ProofSearchInformation object containing information about the performed work, such as the number of rules applied.

    • start

      public ApplyStrategyInfo<Proof,Goal> start(Proof proof, ImmutableList<Goal> goals)
      Description copied from interface: ProverCore
      Starts a proof search for a list of goals using the specified proof instance and strategy settings provided by the proof instance.
      Parameters:
      proof - The ProofObject representing the proof instance.
      goals - An ImmutableList of ProofGoal objects to prove.
      Returns:
      An ProofSearchInformation object containing information about the performed work, such as the number of rules applied.

    • start

      public ApplyStrategyInfo<Proof,Goal> start(Proof proof, ImmutableList<Goal> goals, Object strategySettings)
      Description copied from interface: ProverCore
      Starts a proof search for a list of goals using the specified proof instance and the provided strategy settings.
      Parameters:
      proof - The ProofObject representing the proof instance.
      goals - An ImmutableList of ProofGoal objects to prove.
      strategySettings - The strategy settings to use during the proof search.
      Returns:
      An ProofSearchInformation object containing information about the performed work, such as the number of rules applied.

    • start

      public ApplyStrategyInfo<Proof,Goal> start(Proof proof, ImmutableList<Goal> goals, int maxSteps, long timeout, boolean stopAtFirstNonCloseableGoal)
      Description copied from interface: ProverCore
      Starts a proof search for a list of goals using the specified proof instance, with configurable options for maximum steps, timeout, and early termination.
      Parameters:
      proof - The ProofObject representing the proof instance.
      goals - An ImmutableList of ProofGoal objects to prove.
      maxSteps - The maximum number of rule applications to perform.
      timeout - The maximum duration (in milliseconds) to perform the proof search.
      stopAtFirstNonCloseableGoal - If true, the proof search stops at the first encountered non-closeable goal.
      Returns:
      An ProofSearchInformation object containing information about the performed work, such as the number of rules applied.

    • updateBuiltInRuleIndex

      protected final @Nullable RuleApp updateBuiltInRuleIndex(Goal goal, @Nullable RuleApp app)
      Description copied from class: DefaultProver

      This is currently a hook method for the JavaDL prover as according to a comment the built-in-rule index is not updated when rules are applied.

      Ultimately, this has to be fixed in the listener structure of JavaDL. But for the moment we move it up. Other implementations should implement this method as an empty method.

      Specified by:
      updateBuiltInRuleIndex in class DefaultProver<Proof,Goal>
      Parameters:
      goal - the Goal currently being processed
      app - the RuleApp to be applied next (if null, the built-in-rule index is updated and queried whether a built-in rule is applicable)
      Returns:
      the next RuleApp to apply, or null if no rule is applicable

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Description copied from interface: ProverCore
      Clears all resources and state associated with the current proof session. This is typically called when a proof obligation is abandoned to prevent memory leaks and ensure that references to the proof are reset.

    • hasBeenInterrupted

      public boolean hasBeenInterrupted()
      Description copied from interface: ProverCore
      Indicates whether the last proof run was interrupted due to an InterruptedException. This flag allows detection of interruptions even when the exception has been swallowed by the system.
      Returns:
      true if the last run was interrupted, false otherwise.
